sourceMovie.title: 3 Days to Kill
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto 3 Days to Kill (2014) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Ultimatum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 3 Days to Kill clicked for you, The Bourne Ultimatum (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: In the Line of Fire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 3 Days to Kill clicked for you, In the Line of Fire (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after 3 Days to Kill?
faq.answer: Try The Bourne Ultimatum, In the Line of Fire, Clear and Present Danger—each shares DNA with 3 Days to Kill while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is 3 Days to Kill?
faq.answer: 3 Days to Kill (2014) is best described as Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is 3 Days to Kill worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
